{\n316\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n317\t\tif m.running {\n318\t\t\tm.mu.Unlock()\n319\t\t\treturn\n320\t\t}\n321\t\tm.running = true\n322\t\tm.ctx, m.cancel = {\n348\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n349\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n350\t\n351\t\tif m.cancel != nil {\n352\t\t\tm.cancel()\n353\t\t}\n354\t\tm.running = false\n355\t}\n356\t\n357\t// checkQuality checks zone quality Package guidedtroubleshoot provides proactive contextual help and\n// post-feedback explanations for Spaxel users.\npackage guidedtroubleshoot\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// Qualifying settings keys that trigger repeated-edit hints\nvar QualifyingSettingsKeys = map[string]bool{\n\t\"delta_rms_threshold\":    true,\n\t\"breathing_sensitivity\":  true,\n\t\"tau_s\":                  true,\n\t\"fresnel_decay\":          true,\n\t\"n_subcarriers\":          true,\n\t\"motion_threshold\":       true,\n}\n\n// EditTracker tracks edits to settings keys for repeated-edit hints.\ntype EditTracker struct {\n\tmu         sync.RWMutex\n\tedits      map[string]*editState // key -> edit state\n\tEditWindow time.Duration         // How long edits are grouped together (default 60 minutes)\n}\n\n// editState tracks the edit count and last edit time for a settings key.\ntype editState struct {\n\tcount      int\n\tlastEdit   time.Time\n\tfirstEdit  time.Time\n\thintShown  bool\n\thintReset  time.Time // When to allow showing hint again (24h cooldown)\n}\n\n// NewEditTracker creates a new edit tracker.\nfunc NewEditTracker() *EditTracker {\n\treturn &EditTracker{\n\t\tedits:      make(map[string]*editState),\n\t\tEditWindow: 60 * time.Minute,\n\t}\n}\n\n// RecordEdit records an edit to a settings key.\n// Returns (hintPending bool, hintReset bool).\n// hintPending is true if the edit count has reached the threshold.\n// hintReset is true if the hint reset time has passed and hint can be shown again.\nfunc (t *EditTracker) RecordEdit(key string) (bool, bool) {\n\tif !QualifyingSettingsKeys[key] {\n\t\treturn false, false\n\t}\n\n\tt.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer t.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tnow := time.Now()\n\tstate, exists := t.edits[key]\n\n\tif !exists {\n\t\tstate = &editState{\n\t\t\tfirstEdit: now,\n\t\t}\n\t\tt.edits[key] = state\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if we're past the reset window (24 hours cooldown)\n\tif !state.hintReset.IsZero() && now.After(state.hintReset) {\n\t\t// Reset the counter after cooldown\n\t\tstate.count = 0\n\t\tstate.hintReset = time.Time{}\n\t\tstate.hintShown = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if edits are within the edit window (default 60 minutes)\n\teditWin := t.EditWindow\n\tif editWin <= 0 {\n\t\teditWin = 60 * time.Minute\n\t}\n\twindowStart := now.Add(-editWin)\n\tif state.lastEdit.Before(windowStart) {\n\t\t// Edits are outside the window, reset counter and hint flag\n\t\tstate.count = 1\n\t\tstate.firstEdit = now\n\t\tstate.hintShown = false\n\t} else {\n\t\tstate.count++\n\t}\n\n\tstate.lastEdit = now\n\n\t// Trigger hint at 3 edits within the window\n\tif state.count >= 3 && !state.hintShown {\n\t\treturn true, false\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, false\n}\n\n// MarkHintShown marks that a hint has been displayed for a key.\n// Sets a 24-hour cooldown before the hint can be shown again.\nfunc (t *EditTracker) MarkHintShown(key string) {\n\tt.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer t.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tstate := t.edits[key]\n\tif state != nil {\n\t\tstate.hintShown = true\n\t\tstate.hintReset = time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetEditCount returns the current edit count for a key.\nfunc (t *EditTracker) GetEditCount(key string) int {\n\tt.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer t.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tstate := t.edits[key]\n\tif state == nil {\n\t\treturn 0\n\t}\n\treturn state.count\n}\n\n// Reset resets all edit tracking (used for testing).\nfunc (t *EditTracker) Reset() {\n\tt.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer t.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tt.edits = make(map[string]*editState)\n}\n\n// ZoneQualityTracker tracks detection quality per zone.\ntype ZoneQualityTracker struct {\n\tmu     sync.RWMutex\n\tzones  map[int]*zoneQualityState // zone ID -> quality state\n\tgetAll func() ([]ZoneInfo, error)\n}\n\n// ZoneInfo represents information about a zone.\ntype ZoneInfo struct {\n\tID          int\n\tName        string\n\tQuality     float64 // 0-100\n\tLastUpdated time.Time\n}\n\n// zoneQualityState tracks the quality state for a single zone.\ntype zoneQualityState struct {\n\tzoneID           int\n\tquality          float64\n\tfirstPoorTime    time.Time // When quality first dropped below 60%\n\tlastPoorTime     time.Time\n\tbannerShown      bool\n\tresolvedCount    int\n\thysteresis       float64 // For quality improvements\n}\n\nconst (\n\tQualityThreshold     = 60.0 // Quality below this triggers issues\n\tQualityRecovery     = 70.0 // Quality above this marks recovery\n\tPoorQualityDuration = 24 * time.Hour\n)\n\n// NewZoneQualityTracker creates a new zone quality tracker.\nfunc NewZoneQualityTracker(getAll func() ([]ZoneInfo, error)) *ZoneQualityTracker {\n\treturn &ZoneQualityTracker{\n\t\tzones:  make(map[int]*zoneQualityState),\n\t\tgetAll: getAll,\n\t}\n}\n\n// UpdateQuality updates the quality for a zone.\n// Returns (shouldShowBanner bool, issueResolved bool).\nfunc (t *ZoneQualityTracker) UpdateQuality(zoneID int, quality float64, timestamp time.Time) (bool, bool) {\n\tif quality < 0 || quality > 100 {\n\t\treturn false, false\n\t}\n\n\tt.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer t.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tstate := t.zones[zoneID]\n\tif state == nil {\n\t\tstate = &zoneQualityState{\n\t\t\tzoneID:    zoneID,\n\t\t\tquality:   quality,\n\t\t\thysteresis: quality,\n\t\t}\n\t\t// If initial quality is already poor, set firstPoorTime\n\t\tif quality < QualityThreshold {\n\t\t\tstate.firstPoorTime = timestamp\n\t\t\tstate.lastPoorTime = timestamp\n\t\t}\n\t\tt.zones[zoneID] = state\n\t\treturn false, false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for quality degradation\n\tif quality < QualityThreshold && state.quality >= QualityThreshold {\n\t\t// Quality just dropped below threshold\n\t\tstate.firstPoorTime = timestamp\n\t\tstate.lastPoorTime = timestamp\n\t} else if quality < QualityThreshold {\n\t\t// Still poor quality\n\t\tstate.lastPoorTime = timestamp\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for recovery (with hysteresis to prevent flapping)\n\t// Recovery requires quality >= QualityRecovery (70%), which is higher than\n\t// the poor threshold (60%), providing hysteresis to prevent flapping.\n\t// Only mark resolved if the zone was actually in prolonged poor quality (>24h).\n\tif quality >= QualityRecovery &&\n\t\t!state.firstPoorTime.IsZero() &&\n\t\ttimestamp.Sub(state.firstPoorTime) > PoorQualityDuration {\n\t\tstate.bannerShown = false\n\t\tstate.resolvedCount = 0\n\t\tstate.firstPoorTime = time.Time{}\n\t\tstate.quality = quality\n\t\tstate.hysteresis = quality\n\t\treturn false, true // Issue resolved\n\t}\n\n\tstate.quality = quality\n\tstate.hysteresis = quality\n\n\t// Check if we should show banner (poor quality for >24h and not yet shown)\n\tif quality < QualityThreshold &&\n\t\t!state.firstPoorTime.IsZero() &&\n\t\ttimestamp.Sub(state.firstPoorTime) > PoorQualityDuration &&\n\t\t!state.bannerShown {\n\t\treturn true, false\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, false\n}\n\n// MarkBannerShown marks that a banner has been shown for a zone.\nfunc (t *ZoneQualityTracker) MarkBannerShown(zoneID int) {\n\tt.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer t.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tstate := t.zones[zoneID]\n\tif state != nil {\n\t\tstate.bannerShown = true\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetZonesWithPoorQuality returns zones with quality < 60% for >24 hours.\nfunc (t *ZoneQualityTracker) GetZonesWithPoorQuality() []int {\n\tt.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer t.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tvar zones []int\n\tnow := time.Now()\n\n\tfor _, state := range t.zones {\n\t\tif state.quality < QualityThreshold &&\n\t\t\t!state.firstPoorTime.IsZero() &&\n\t\t\tnow.Sub(state.firstPoorTime) > PoorQualityDuration {\n\t\t\tzones = append(zones, state.zoneID)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn zones\n}\n\n// Reset clears all zone quality tracking (used for testing).\nfunc (t *ZoneQualityTracker) Reset() {\n\tt.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer t.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tt.zones = make(map[int]*zoneQualityState)\n}\n\n// Manager coordinates all guided troubleshooting features.\ntype Manager struct {\n\teditTracker        *EditTracker\n\tqualityTracker     *ZoneQualityTracker\n\tdiscoveryTracker   *DiscoveryTracker\n\tfleetNotifier      *FleetNotifier\n\tmu                 sync.RWMutex\n\trunning            bool\n\tctx                context.Context\n\tcancel             context.CancelFunc\n\tcheckInterval      time.Duration\n\tonQualityIssue     func(zoneID error)\n\tGetNodeLastSeen     func(mac string) time.Time\n}\n\n// NewManager creates a new guided troubleshooting manager.\nfunc NewManager(cfg ManagerConfig) *Manager {\n\tif cfg.CheckInterval == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.CheckInterval = 5 * time.Minute\n\t}\n\n\treturn &Manager{\n\t\teditTracker:      NewEditTracker(),\n\t\tqualityTracker:   NewZoneQualityTracker(cfg.GetAllZones),\n\t\tdiscoveryTracker: NewDiscoveryTracker(),\n\t\tcheckInterval:    cfg.CheckInterval,\n\t}\n}\n\n// Run starts the background check loop.\nfunc (m *Manager) Run(ctx context.Context) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tif m.running {\n\t\tm.mu.Unlock()\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tm.running = true\n\tm.ctx, m.cancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\tm.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(m.checkInterval)\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] guidedtroubleshoot: manager started (interval: %v)\", m.checkInterval)\n\n\t// Initial check\n\tm.checkQuality()\n\tm.checkOfflineNodes()\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-m.ctx.Done():\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] guidedtroubleshoot: manager stopped\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\tm.checkQuality()\n\t\t\tm.checkOfflineNodes()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// Stop stops the background check loop.\nfunc (m *Manager) Stop() {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif m.cancel != nil {\n\t\tm.cancel()\n\t}\n\tm.running = false\n}\n\n// checkQuality checks zone quality and triggers callbacks.\nfunc (m *Manager) checkQuality() {\n\tm.mu.RLock()\n\tgetAll := m.qualityTracker.getAll\n\tm.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif getAll == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tzones, err := getAll()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] guidedtroubleshoot: failed to get zones: %v\", err)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tnow := time.Now()\n\tfor _, zone := range zones {\n\t\tshouldShow, resolved := m.qualityTracker.UpdateQuality(zone.ID, zone.Quality, now)\n\n\t\tif shouldShow && m.onQualityIssue != nil {\n\t\t\tm.onQualityIssue(zone.ID, zone.Quality)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif resolved && m.onQualityIssue != nil {\n\t\t\t// Could trigger a \"resolved\" notification\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] guidedtroubleshoot: zone %d quality recovered to %.1f%%\", zone.ID, zone.Quality)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// checkOfflineNodes checks all tracked offline nodes and triggers callbacks.\nfunc (m *Manager) checkOfflineNodes() {\n\tm.mu.RLock()\n\tnotifier := m.fleetNotifier\n\tm.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif notifier != nil {\n\t\tnotifier.CheckOfflineNodes()\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetFleetNotifier sets the fleet notifier for tracking node offline events.\nfunc (m *Manager) SetFleetNotifier(notifier *FleetNotifier) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\tm.fleetNotifier = notifier\n}\n\n// SetOnQualityIssue sets the callback for quality issues.\nfunc (m *Manager) SetOnQualityIssue(fn func(zoneID int, quality float64)) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\tm.onQualityIssue = fn\n}\n\n// SetOnNodeOffline sets the callback for node offline events.\nfunc (m *Manager) SetOnNodeOffline(fn func(mac string, offlineDuration time.Duration)) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\tm.onNodeOffline = fn\n}\n\n// SetOnCalibrationComplete sets the callback for calibration completion.\nfunc (m *Manager) SetOnCalibrationComplete(fn func(zoneID int, qualityBefore, qualityAfter float64)) {\n\tm.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n\tm.onCalibrationComplete = fn\n}\n\n// RecordSettingsEdit records an edit to a settings key.\nfunc (m 'active' : ''} ${i < activeGuide.currentStep ? 'completed' : ''}\"></div>\n294\t                        `).join('')}\n295\t                    </div>\n296\t\n297\t                    <div class=\"help-step\">\n298\t                        <h4 </button>\n321\t                    `}\n322\t\n323\t                    ${step.action ? `\n324\t                        <button class=\"help-btn help-btn-action\" 'toggle-gdop': 'Show GDOP',\n349\t            'view-timeline': 'View Timeline',\n350\t            'give-feedback': 'Give Feedback',\n351\t            'open-settings': 'Open Settings',\n352\t }\n419\t\n420\t    // ===== Navigation =====\n421\t    function nextStep() {\n422\t        if (!activeGuide) return;\n423\t        if (activeGuide.currentStep < activeGuide.data.steps.length - 1) {\n424\t            activeGuide.currentStep++;\n425\t            renderGuide();\n426\t        }\n427\t    }\n428\t\n429\t    function previousStep() {\n430\t        if (!activeGuide) return;\n431\t        if (activeGuide.currentStep > 0) {\n432\t            activeGuide.currentStep--;\n433\t            renderGuide();\n434\t        }\n435\t    }\n436\t\n437\t    function complete() {\n438\t    saveDismissedGuides();\n442\t        }\n443\t        dismiss();\n444\t    }\n445\t\n446\t    function dismiss() {\n447\t        helpContainer.classList.remove('visible');\n448\t        activeGuide = null;\n449\t        helpPanelVisible = false;\n450\t    }\n451\t\n452\t    // ===== Persistence =====\n453\t    function saveDismissedGuides() {\n454\t        try {\n455\t help-btn-secondary\" onclick=\"GuidedHelp.previousStep()\">\n                            ← Back\n                        </button>\n                    ` : `\n                        <button ${step.action ? `\n                        <button class=\"help-btn help-btn-action\" onclick=\"GuidedHelp.executeAction('${step.action}')\">\n                            ${getActionLabel(step.action)}\n                        </button>\n                    ` : ''}\n\n                    ${!isLastStep ? `\n                        <button class=\"help-btn help-btn-primary\" onclick=\"GuidedHelp.nextStep()\">\n                            Next →\n                        </button>\n                    ` : `\n                        <button class=\"help-btn help-btn-primary\" onclick=\"GuidedHelp.complete()\">\n                            Got it\n                        </button>\n                    `}\n                </div>\n            </div>\n        `;\n\n        helpContainer.classList.add('visible');\n    }\n\n    function getActionLabel(action) {\n        const labels = {\n            'add-node': 'Add Node',\n            'toggle-gdop': 'Show GDOP',\n            'view-timeline': 'View Timeline',\n            'give-feedback': 'Give Feedback',\n            'open-settings': 'Open Settings',\n            'open-automations': 'Automations',\n            'open-simulator': 'Open Simulator',\n            'create-zone': 'Create Zone',\n            'add-trigger': 'Add Trigger',\n            'view-automations': 'View Automations',\n            'test-webhook': 'Test Webhook',\n            'view-logs': 'View Logs'\n        };\n        return labels[action] || 'Action';\n    }\n\n    function executeAction(action) {\n        switch (action) {\n            case 'add-node':\n                if (window.SpaxelOnboard) {\n                    SpaxelOnboard.start();\n                }\n                break;\n            case 'toggle-gdop':\n                if (window.Placement) {\n                    Placement.toggleGDOP();\n                }\n                break;\n            case 'view-timeline':\n                if (window.SpaxelRouter) {\n                    SpaxelRouter.navigate('timeline');\n                }\n                break;\n            case 'give-feedback':\n                if (window.FeedbackUI) {\n                    FeedbackUI.openForContext(activeGuide.context);\n                }\n                break;\n            case 'open-settings':\n                    SpaxelRouter.navigate('automations');\n                }\n                break;\n            case 'open-simulator':\n                if (window.Simulate) {\n                    Simulate.togglePanel();\n                }\n                break;\n            case 'create-zone':\n                // Open zone editor\n                break;\n            case 'add-trigger':\n                // Open trigger editor\n                break;\n            case 'view-automations':\n                if (window.SpaxelRouter) {\n                    SpaxelRouter.navigate('automations');\n                }\n                break;\n            case 'test-webhook':\n                // Test webhook functionality\n                break;\n            case 'view-logs':\n                // Show automation logs\n                break;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ===== Navigation =====\n    function nextStep() {\n        if (!activeGuide) return;\n        if (activeGuide.currentStep < activeGuide.data.steps.length - 1) {\n            activeGuide.currentStep++;\n            renderGuide();\n        }\n    }\n\n    function previousStep() {\n        if (!activeGuide) return;\n        if (activeGuide.currentStep > 0) {\n            activeGuide.currentStep--;\n            renderGuide();\n        }\n    }\n\n    function complete() {\n        const dontShowAgain = document.getElementById('help-dont-show-again');\n        if (dontShowAgain && dontShowAgain.checked) {\n            dismissedGuides.add(activeGuide.id);\n            saveDismissedGuides();\n        }\n        dismiss();\n    }\n\n    function dismiss() {\n        helpContainer.classList.remove('visible');\n        activeGuide = null;\n        helpPanelVisible = false;\n    }\n\n    // ===== Persistence =====\n    function saveDismissedGuides() {\n        try {\n            localStorage.setItem('spaxel_dismissed_guides', Array.from(dismissedGuides).join(','));\n        } catch (e) {\n            console.warn('Failed to save dismissed guides:', e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    function loadDismissedGuides() {\n        try {\n            const saved = localStorage.getItem('spaxel_dismissed_guides');\n            if (saved) {\n                dismissedGuides = new Set(saved.split(',').filter(id => id));\n            }\n        } catch (e) {\n            console.warn('Failed to load dismissed guides:', e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ===== Proactive Triggers =====\n    function checkProactiveTriggers() {\n        // No nodes\n        if (window.Viz3D && Viz3D.getNodes && Viz3D.getNodes().length === 0) {\n            showGuide('no-nodes');\n        }\n\n        // Poor detection quality\n        const qualityGauge = document.getElementById('quality-value');\n        if (qualityGauge) {\n            const quality = parseInt(qualityGauge.textContent);\n            if (!isNaN(quality) && quality < 60) {\n                showGuide('poor-detection');\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ===== Context Menu Integration =====\n    function addContextualHelp(target, guideId) {\n        // Add help button to * Spaxel Troubleshooting Manager\n *\n * Handles node offline cards, detection quality banners, and\n * calibration completion reinforcement. Subscribes to WebSocket\n * events via TroubleshootManager.handleEvent() called from app.js.\n *\n * Issue state machine per issue key:\n *   DETECTED -> NOTIFIED -> RESOLVED | DISMISSED\n *\n * State is in-memory only — issues re-fire on next event after page refresh.\n */\n\n(function () {\n    'use strict';\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Constants\n    // ============================================\n    var STATES = {\n        DETECTED: 'detected',\n        NOTIFIED: 'notified',\n        RESOLVED: 'resolved',\n        DISMISSED: 'dismissed',\n    };\n\n    var PACKET_CHECK_MS = 5000;   // check link health every 5 s\n    var NO_FRAME_MS = 60000;      // 60 s with no frames = quality issue\n    var RECOVERY_RATIO = 0.8;     // frames resume → resolve if within 80 % of threshold\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Internal State\n    // ============================================\n    var state = {\n        issues: {},       // issueKey -> { state, data, element }\n        checkTimer: null,\n        nodePanelSection: null,\n    };\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Initialization\n    // ============================================\n    function init() {\n        // Create troubleshoot section inside the node panel\n        var panel = document.getElementById('node-panel');\n        if (panel) {\n            state.nodePanelSection = document.createElement('div');\n            state.nodePanelSection.id = 'troubleshoot-section';\n            panel.appendChild(state.nodePanelSection);\n        }\n\n        // Periodic client-side link health check\n        state.checkTimer = setInterval(checkLinkHealth, PACKET_CHECK_MS);\n    }\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Public Event Handler (called from app.js)\n    // ============================================\n    function handleEvent(type, data) {\n        switch (type) {\n            case 'node_disconnected':\n                handleNodeOffline(data);\n                break;\n            case 'node_connected':\n                handleNodeOnline(data);\n                break;\n            case 'low_packet_rate':\n                handleLowPacketRate(data);\n                break;\n            case 'calibration_complete':\n                handleCalibrationComplete(data);\n                break;\n            case 'quality_drop':\n                handleQualityDrop(data);\n                break;\n            case 'repeated_edit':\n                handleRepeatedEdit(data);\n                break;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Node Offline\n    // ============================================\n    function handleNodeOffline(data) {\n        var mac = data.mac;\n        var key = 'offline_' + mac;\n\n        if (state.issues[key]) return; // already tracking\n\n        var issue = { state: STATES.DETECTED, data: data, element: null };\n        state.issues[key] = issue;\n        issue.state = STATES.NOTIFIED;\n        issue.element = renderOfflineCard(mac);\n    }\n\n    function handleNodeOnline(data) {\n        var mac = data.mac;\n        var key = 'offline_' + mac;\n\n        resolveIssue(key);\n\n        // Also clear any quality banners for links involving this node\n        clearQualityIssuesForNode(mac);\n    }\n\n    function renderOfflineCard(mac) {\n        var last4 = mac.slice(-5).replace(':', '');\n        var time = new Date().toLocaleTimeString([], { hour: '2-digit', minute: '2-digit' });\n\n        var card = document.createElement('div');\n        card.className = 'troubleshoot-card troubleshoot-offline-card';\n        card.innerHTML =\n            '<div class=\"troubleshoot-card-header\">' +\n                '<span class=\"troubleshoot-card-icon\">\\u26A0</span>' +\n                '<span>Node <strong>' + escapeAttr(mac) + '</strong> went offline at ' + escapeAttr(time) + '</span>' +\n                '<button class=\"troubleshoot-dismiss\" title=\"Dismiss\">&times;</button>' +\n            '</div>' +\n            '<div class=\"troubleshoot-timeline\">' +\n                '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step\">' +\n                    '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step-num\">1</div>' +\n                    '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step-text\">Check the node\\'s power LED is on (solid green = connected, blinking = attempting WiFi)</div>' +\n                '</div>' +\n                '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step\">' +\n                    '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step-num\">2</div>' +\n                    '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step-text\">If blinking: move the node closer to your WiFi router temporarily</div>' +\n                '</div>' +\n                '<details class=\"troubleshoot-more\">' +\n                    '<summary>More options</summary>' +\n                    '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step\">' +\n                        '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step-num\">3</div>' +\n                        '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step-text\">If the LED blinks rapidly after 5 minutes: the node has lost its WiFi configuration. Connect to <strong>spaxel-' + escapeAttr(last4) + '</strong> WiFi network to reconfigure.</div>' +\n                    '</div>' +\n                    '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step\">' +\n                        '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step-num\">4</div>' +\n                        '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step-text\">If the LED is off: check the power supply and USB cable</div>' +\n                    '</div>' +\n                    '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step\">' +\n                        '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step-num\">5</div>' +\n                        '<div class=\"troubleshoot-step-text\">Still stuck? <button class=\"troubleshoot-reset-btn\" data-mac=\"' + escapeAttr(mac) + '\">Reset to factory defaults</button></div>' +\n                    '</div>' +\n                '</details>' +\n            '</div>';\n\n        // Dismiss\n        card.querySelector('.troubleshoot-dismiss').addEventListener('click', function () {\n            resolveIssue('offline_' + mac);\n        });\n\n        // Factory reset instructions\n        var resetBtn = card.querySelector('.troubleshoot-reset-btn');\n        if (resetBtn) {\n            resetBtn.addEventListener('click', function () {\n                showResetInstructions(mac);\n            });\n        }\n\n        if (state.nodePanelSection) {\n            state.nodePanelSection.appendChild(card);\n        }\n        return card;\n    }\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Factory Reset Instructions Modal\n    // ============================================\n    function showResetInstructions(mac) {\n        var modal = document.createElement('div');\n        modal.className = 'troubleshoot-modal-overlay';\n        modal.innerHTML =\n            '<div class=\"troubleshoot-modal\">' +\n                '<h3>Factory Reset Instructions</h3>' +\n                '<ol class=\"troubleshoot-list\">' +\n                    '<li>Unplug the node from power</li>' +\n                    '<li>Hold the <strong>BOOT</strong> button on the ESP32-S3</li>' +\n                    '<li>While holding BOOT, plug in the USB Rate)\n    // ============================================\n    function handleLowPacketRate(data) {\n        var linkID = data.link_id;\n        var key = 'quality_' + linkID;\n\n        if (state.issues[key]) return;\n\n        state.issues[key] = { state: STATES.NOTIFIED, data: data, element: null };\n        state.issues[key].element = renderQualityBanner(data);\n    }\n\n    function renderQualityBanner(data) {\n        var banner = document.createElement('div');\n        banner.className = 'troubleshoot-quality-banner';\n        var safeId = (data.link_id || link.lastFrame;\n\n            if (elapsed > NO_FRAME_MS) {\n                // No frames for over 60 s — flag quality issue\n                var key = 'quality_' + linkID;\n                if (!state.issues[key]) {\n                    handleLowPacketRate({\n                        link_id: linkID,\n                        node_mac: link.nodeMAC,\n                        peer_mac: link.peerMAC,\n                    });\n                }\n            } else if (elapsed < NO_FRAME_MS * RECOVERY_RATIO) {\n                // Frames resumed — auto-resolve\n                var key = 'quality_' + linkID;\n                if (state.issues[key]) {\n                    resolveIssue(key);\n                }\n            }\n        });\n    }\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Calibration Complete\n    // ============================================\n    function handleCalibrationComplete(data) {\n        if (!data) return;\n\n        // Show positive reinforcement message\n        showCalibrationReinforcement(data);\n    }\n\n    function showCalibrationReinforcement(data) {\n        var improvement = Math.round((data.quality_after || 0) - (data.quality_before || 0));\n        var improvementText = improvement > 0 ? '+' + improvement : Math.round(improvement);\n        var encouragement = '';\n\n        if (improvement > 20) {\n            encouragement = 'Excellent! That\\'s a significant improvement.';\n        } else if (improvement > 10) {\n            encouragement = 'Great progress! Detection is much more reliable now.';\n        } else if (improvement > 0) {\n            encouragement = 'Getting better. The system will continue to refine baseline over time.';\n        } else {\n            encouragement = 'Baseline has been updated. The system needs more data to adapt to this environment.';\n        }\n\n        var card = document.createElement('div');\n        card.className = 'troubleshoot-card troubleshoot-success-card';\n        card.innerHTML =\n            '<div class=\"troubleshoot-card-header\">' +\n                '<span class=\"troubleshoot-card-icon\">\\u2714</span>' +\n                '<span><strong>Re-baseline +\n                '<p class=\"troubleshoot-success-message\">' + encouragement + '</p>' +\n                '<div class=\"troubleshoot-metrics\">' +\n                    '<span>Quality: ' + (card.parentNode) card.parentNode.removeChild(card);\n        });\n\n        if (state.nodePanelSection) {\n            state.nodePanelSection.appendChild(card);\n        }\n\n        // Auto-dismiss after 10 seconds\n        setTimeout(function() {\n            if (card.parentNode) {\n                card.classList.add('troubleshoot-card-fadeout');\n                setTimeout(function() {\n                    if (card.parentNode) card.parentNode.removeChild(card);\n                }, 500);\n            }\n        }, 10000);\n    }\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Quality Drop Detection\n    // ============================================\n    function handleQualityDrop(data) {\n        if (!data || !data.zone_id) return;\n\n        var key = 'quality_' + data.zone_id;\n        if (state.issues[key]) return;\n\n        state.issues[key] = { state: STATES.NOTIFIED, data: data, element: null };\n        state.issues[key].element = handleGetLinkDiagnostics returns diagnostic information for a specific link.\n74\tfunc (h *GuidedHandler) handleGetLinkDiagnostics(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n75\t\tlinkID := chi.URLParam(r, \"linkID\")\n76\t\tif linkID == \"\" {\n77\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"missing link ID\")\n78\t\t\treturn\n79\t\t}\n80\t\n81\t\t// Parse optional timestamp parameter\n82\t\tvar timestamp time.Time\n83\t\ttimestampStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"timestamp\")\n84\t\tif timestampStr != \"\" {\n85\t\t\tif ts, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, timestampStr); err == nil {\n86\t\t\t\ttimestamp = ts\n87\t\t\t} else if ms, err := time.Parse(timestampStr, \"20060102150405\"); err == nil {\n88\t\t\t\ttimestamp = ms\n89\t\t\t}\n90\t\t}\n91\t\n92\t\tvar diagnosis *diagnostics.Diagnosis\n93\t\tvar diagnoses []diagnostics.Diagnosis\n94\t\n95\t\tif h.diagnosticsHandler != nil {\n96\t\t\tif !timestamp.IsZero() {\n97\t\t\t\tdiagnosis = h.diagnosticsHandler.GetDiagnosticFor(linkID, timestamp)\n98\t\t\t}\n99\t\t\tdiagnoses = h.diagnosticsHandler.GetDiagnoses(linkID)\n100\t\t}\n101\t\n102\t\t// Get current link health info\n103\t\thealth := h.getCurrentLinkHealth(linkID)\n104\t\n105\t\tresponse := map[string]interface{}{\n106\t\t\t\"link_id\":  linkID,\n107\t\t\t\"diagnosis\": diagnosis,\n108\t\t\t\"diagnoses\": diagnoses,\n109\t\t\t\"health\":   health,\n110\t\t}\n111\t\n112\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t// getCurrentLinkHealth returns the current health snapshot for a link\n116\tfunc (h *GuidedHandler) getCurrentLinkHealth(linkID string) map[string]interface{} {\n117\t\t// Try to get current health from the link weather diagnostics\n118\t\tif h.diagnosticsHandler == nil {\n119\t\t\treturn nil\n120\t\t}\n121\t\n122\t\t// Get diagnoses and extract the most recent diagnosis with actionable info\n123\t\tdiagnoses := h.diagnosticsHandler.GetDiagnoses(linkID)\n124\t\tif len(diagnoses) == 0 {\n125\t\t\treturn map[string]interface{}{\n126\t\t\t\t\"message\": \"No diagnostic data available yet. Link needs more observation time.\",\n127\t\t\t}\n128\t\t}\n129\t\n130\t\t// Get the most recent diagnosis\n131\t\tmostRecent := diagnoses[0]\n132\t\n133\t\t// Build health map with current metrics\n134\t\thealth := map[string]interface{}{\n135\t\t\t\"packet_rate\":      20.0, // Default expected rate\n136\t\t\t\"snr\":              0.5,  // Default SNR\n137\t\t\t\"phase_stability\":  0.5,  // Default stability\n138\t\t\t\"drift_rate\":       0.0,  // No drift\n139\t\t\t\"composite_score\":  mostRecent.ConfidenceScore,\n140\t\t}\n141\t\n142\t\t// If the diagnosis has specific rule info, we can infer health metrics\n143\t\tswitch mostRecent.RuleID {\n144\t\tcase \"wifi_congestion_distance\":\n145\t\t\thealth[\"packet_rate\"] = 12.0 // Low packet rate\n146\t\t\thealth[\"composite_score\"] = 0.5\n147\t\tcase \"metal_interference\":\n148\t\t\thealth[\"phase_stability\"] = 0.7 // High instability\n149\t\t\thealth[\"composite_score\"] = 0.4\n150\t\tcase \"environmental_change\":\n151\t\t\thealth[\"drift_rate\"] = 0.06 // High drift\n152\t\t\thealth[\"composite_score\"] = 0.6\n153\t\tcase \"fresnel_blockage\", \"fresnel_blockage_heuristic\":\n154\t\t\thealth[\"composite_score\"] = 0.5\n155\t\tcase \"periodic_interference\":\n156\t\t\thealth[\"phase_stability\"] = 0.6\n157\t\t\thealth[\"composite_score\"] = 0.55\n158\t\t}\n159\t\n160\t\t// Add explanation text\n161\t\tif mostRecent.Detail != \"\" {\n162\t\t\thealth[\"explanation\"] = mostRecent.Detail\n163\t\t}\n164\t\tif mostRecent.Advice != \"\" {\n165\t\t\thealth[\"advice\"] = mostRecent.Advice\n166\t\t}\n167\t\n168\t\treturn health\n169\t}\n170\t\n171\t// handleGetIssues returns all active guided troubleshooting issues.\n172\tfunc (h Detection is much more reliable now.\"\n314\t\t} else if improvement > 0 {\n315\t\t\tresponse[\"encouragement\"] = \"Getting better. The system will continue to refine baseline over time.\"\n316\t\t} else {\n317\t\t\tresponse[\"encouragement\"] = \"Baseline has been updated. link.\nfunc (h *GuidedHandler) handleGetLinkDiagnostics(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tlinkID := chi.URLParam(r, \"linkID\")\n\tif linkID == \"\" {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"missing link ID\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse optional timestamp parameter\n\tvar timestamp time.Time\n\ttimestampStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"timestamp\")\n\tif timestampStr != \"\" {\n\t\tif ts, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, timestampStr); err == nil {\n\t\t\ttimestamp = ts\n\t\t} else if ms, err := time.Parse(timestampStr, \"20060102150405\"); err == nil {\n\t\t\ttimestamp = ms\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tvar diagnosis *diagnostics.Diagnosis\n\tvar diagnoses []diagnostics.Diagnosis\n\n\tif h.diagnosticsHandler != nil {\n\t\tif !timestamp.IsZero() {\n\t\t\tdiagnosis = h.diagnosticsHandler.GetDiagnosticFor(linkID, timestamp)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdiagnoses = h.diagnosticsHandler.GetDiagnoses(linkID)\n\t}\n\n\t// Get current link health info\n\thealth := h.getCurrentLinkHealth(linkID)\n\n\tresponse := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"link_id\":  linkID,\n\t\t\"diagnosis\": diagnosis,\n\t\t\"diagnoses\": diagnoses,\n\t\t\"health\":   health,\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n}\n\n// getCurrentLinkHealth returns the current health snapshot for a link\nfunc (h *GuidedHandler) getCurrentLinkHealth(linkID string) map[string]interface{} {\n\t// Try to get current health from the link weather diagnostics\n\tif h.diagnosticsHandler == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Get diagnoses and extract the most recent diagnosis with actionable info\n\tdiagnoses := h.diagnosticsHandler.GetDiagnoses(linkID)\n\tif len(diagnoses) == 0 {\n\t\treturn map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"message\": \"No diagnostic data available yet. Link needs more observation time.\",\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the most recent diagnosis\n\tmostRecent := diagnoses[0]\n\n\t// Build health map with current metrics\n\thealth := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"packet_rate\":      20.0, // Default expected rate\n\t\t\"snr\":              0.5,  // Default SNR\n\t\t\"phase_stability\":  0.5,  // Default stability\n\t\t\"drift_rate\":       0.0,  // No drift\n\t\t\"composite_score\":  mostRecent.ConfidenceScore,\n\t}\n\n\t// If the diagnosis has specific rule info, we can infer health metrics\n\tswitch mostRecent.RuleID {\n\tcase \"wifi_congestion_distance\":\n\t\thealth[\"packet_rate\"] = 12.0 // Low packet rate\n\t\thealth[\"composite_score\"] = 0.5\n\tcase \"metal_interference\":\n\t\thealth[\"phase_stability\"] = 0.7 // High instability\n\t\thealth[\"composite_score\"] = 0.4\n\tcase \"environmental_change\":\n\t\thealth[\"drift_rate\"] = 0.06 // High drift\n\t\thealth[\"composite_score\"] = 0.6\n\tcase \"fresnel_blockage\", \"fresnel_blockage_heuristic\":\n\t\thealth[\"composite_score\"] = 0.5\n\tcase \"periodic_interference\":\n\t\thealth[\"phase_stability\"] = 0.6\n\t\thealth[\"composite_score\"] = 0.55\n\t}\n\n\t// Add explanation text\n\tif mostRecent.Detail != \"\" {\n\t\thealth[\"explanation\"] = mostRecent.Detail\n\t}\n\tif mostRecent.Advice != \"\" {\n\t\thealth[\"advice\"] = mostRecent.Advice\n\t}\n\n\treturn health\n}\n\n// handleGetIssues returns all active guided troubleshooting issues.\nfunc (h *GuidedHandler) handleGetIssues(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif h.guidedMgr == nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\"issues\": []interface{}{}})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tvar issues []map[string]interface{}\n\n\t// Quality issues\n\tpoorZones := h.guidedMgr.GetZonesWithPoorQuality()\n\tfor _, zoneID := range poorZones {\n\t\tzoneName := \"Unknown Zone\"\n\t\tzoneQuality := 0.0\n\n\t\tif h.zonesHandler != nil {\n\t\t\tif zone, err := h.getZoneByID(zoneID); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\tzoneName = zone[\"name\"].(string)\n\t\t\t\tif q, ok := zone[\"quality\"].(float64); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tzoneQuality = q\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tissues = append(issues, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"type\":        \"quality_drop\",\n\t\t\t\"zone_id\":     zoneID,\n\t\t\t\"zone_name\":   zoneName,\n\t\t\t\"quality\":     zoneQuality,\n\t\t\t\"severity\":    \"warning\",\n\t\t\t\"title\":       \"Detection quality has degraded in \" + zoneName,\n\t\t\t\"description\": \"Detection quality in \" + zoneName + \" has been below 60% for over 24 hours. This may indicate node placement issues or environmental changes.\",\n\t\t\t\"actions\": []map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t{\"label\": \"Check node connectivity\", \"action\": \"connectivity\"},\n\t\t\t\t{\"label\": \"View link health\", \"action\": \"link_health\"},\n\t\t\t\t{\"label\": \"Re-baseline links\", \"action\": \"rebaseline\"},\n\t\t\t\t{\"label\": \"Run guided diagnostics\", \"action\": \"diagnostics\"},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\"issues\": issues})\n}\n\n// handleDismissQualityIssue dismisses a quality banner for a zone.\nfunc (h *GuidedHandler) handleDismissQualityIssue(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tzoneID := chi.URLParam(r, \"zoneId\")\n\tvar zoneIDInt int\n\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(zoneID), &zoneIDInt); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid request body\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tvar response map[string]interface{}\n\n\tswitch req.FeedbackType {\n\tcase \"incorrect\":\n\t\tresponse = map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"type\":    \"adjustment\",\n\t\t\t\"title\":   \"Adjusting detection threshold\",\n\t\t\t\"message\": \"I've slightly raised the detection threshold for the contributing links. If this keeps happening at this time of day, my hourly baseline will adapt within a few days. You can also adjust sensitivity manually in Settings.\",\n\t\t\t\"actions\": []map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t{\"label\": \"Open Settings\", \"action\": \"open_settings\"},\n\t\t\t\t{\"label\": \"View Link Details\", \"action\": \"view_links\"},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\n\tcase \"correct\":\n\t\tresponse = map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"type\":    \"confirmation\",\n\t\t\t\"title\":   \"Detection confirmed\",\n\t\t\t\"message\": \"Thanks for confirming! This helps improve detection accuracy over time.\",\n\t\t}\n\n\tdefault:\n\t\tresponse = map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"type\":    \"info\",\n\t\t\t\"message\": \"Feedback recorded\",\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n}\n\n// handleCalibrationComplete reports calibration completion and triggers reinforcement.\nfunc (h *GuidedHandler) handleCalibrationComplete(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req struct {\n\t\tZoneID         int     `json:\"zone_id\"`\n\t\tQualityBefore  float64 `json:\"quality_before\"`\n\t\tQualityAfter   float64 `json:\"quality_after\"`\n\t\tLinksCalibrated int     `json:\"links_calibrated\"`\n\t}\n\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid request body\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif h.guidedMgr != nil {\n\t\th.guidedMgr.TriggerCalibrationComplete(req.ZoneID, req.QualityBefore, req.QualityAfter)\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate improvement\n\timprovement := req.QualityAfter - req.QualityBefore\n\timprovementPct := int(improvement)\n\n\tresponse := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"type\":         \"calibration_complete\",\n\t\t\"title\":        \"Re-baseline complete\",\n\t\t\"message\":      \"Detection quality in this zone has improved.\",\n\t\t\"improvement\":  improvementPct,\n\t\t\"quality_after\": req.QualityAfter,\n\t\t\"links\":        req.LinksCalibrated,\n\t}\n\n\t// Add encouraging message based on improvement\n\tif improvement > 20 {\n\t\tresponse[\"encouragement\"] = \"Excellent! Detection is much more reliable now.\"\n\t} else if improvement > 0 {\n\t\tresponse[\"encouragement\"] = \"Getting better. The system will continue to refine baseline over time.\"\n\t} else {\n\t\tresponse[\"encouragement\"] = \"Baseline has been updated. The system needs more data to adapt to this environment.\"\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n}\n\n// handleGetNodeTroubleshoot returns troubleshooting steps for an offline node.\nfunc (h *GuidedHandler) handleGetNodeTroubleshoot(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tmac := chi.URLParam(r, \"mac\")\n\n\t// Get node info\n\tvar nodeName, nodeRole string\n\tvar offlineDuration float64\n\n\tif h.nodesHandler != nil {\n\t\tnodes, err := h.nodesHandler.(interface {\n\t\t\tGetAllNodes() ([]map[string]interface{}, error)\n\t\t}).GetAllNodes()\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\t\t\tif nodeMAC, ok := node[\"mac\"].(string); ok && nodeMAC == mac {\n\t\t\t\t\tnodeName = node[\"name\"].(string)\n\t\t\t\t\tnodeRole = node[\"role\"].(string)\n\t\t\t\t\t// Calculate offline duration from last_seen_ms\n\t\t\t\t\tif lastSeenMs, ok := node[\"last_seen_ms\"].(int64); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// Calculate approximate duration\n\t\t\t\t\t\tofflineDuration = float64(time.Now().UnixMilli()-lastSeenMs) / 1000 / 60 // in minutes\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Create troubleshooting steps\n\tsteps := []map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\t\"step\":        1,\n\t\t\t\"title\":       \"Check power connection\",\n\t\t\t\"description\": \"Verify the node's USB cable is securely connected and the power LED is on (solid green = connected, blinking = attempting WiFi).\",\n\t\t\t\"actions\":     []string{\"Visually inspect the node\", \"Check the USB cable connection\"},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\t\"step\":        2,\n\t\t\t\"title\":       \"Check WiFi connectivity\",\n\t\t\t\"description\": \"If the LED is blinking, the node is having trouble connecting to WiFi. ok {\n\t\tzone, err := zg.GetZone(id)\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\treturn zone, nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Fall back to getting all zones\n\ttype allZonesGetter interface {\n\t\tGetAllZones() ([]map[string]interface{}, error)\n\t}\n\n\tif azg, ok := h.zonesHandler.(allZonesGetter); ok && int(zoneID) == id {\n\t\t\t\treturn zone, nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil, ErrZoneNotFound\n}\n\n// ErrZoneNotFound is returned when a zone cannot be found.\nvar ErrZoneNotFound = &HTTPError{StatusCode: 404, Message: \"zone not found\"}\n\n// HTTPError represents an HTTP error with a status code and message.\ntype HTTPError struct {\n\tStatusCode int\n\tMessage    string\n}\n\nfunc (e *HTTPError) Error() string {\n\treturn e.Message\n}\n\n// handleGetTooltip returns the tooltip for a feature if it should be shown.\nfunc (h *GuidedHandler) handleGetTooltip(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif h.guidedMgr == nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\"show\": false})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tfeatureID := chi.URLParam(r, \"featureId\")\n\tif featureID == \"\" {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"missing feature ID\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tshouldShow := h.guidedMgr.ShouldShowTooltip(featureID)\n\tif !shouldShow {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\"show\": false})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// GetTooltip returns package-specific Tooltip types; Complex CSI computation: int8 I/Q → float64 complex → amplitude/phase\n43\t  - RSSI normalization (AGC compensation): `norm = 10^((rssi_ref - rssi)/20)`\n44\t  - Spatial phase unwrapping: correct 2π jumps between adjacent subcarriers\n45\t  - Linear regression (OLS): fit `phase = a*k + b` over data subcarriers\n46\t  - STO/CFO removal: residual phase = unwrapped - (slope × k + intercept)\n47\t  - HT20 subcarrier map: 64 total, 46 data (excluding null, guard, pilot)\n48\t\n49\t- **Baseline system (`signal/baseline.go`):**\n50\t  - EMA baseline per link per subcarrier: `baseline = α×amplitude + (1-α)×baseline`\n51\t  - α = dt / (τ + dt) ≈ 0.0033 for dt=0.1s, τ=30s\n52\t  - Motion-gated updates: only update when smoothDeltaRMS < 0.05\n53\t  - Confidence scoring: 0.3 for stale baselines (>7 days), asymptotically → 1.0\n54\t  - Snapshot/restore for SQLite persistence\n55\t  - BaselineManager for multi-link coordination\n56\t\n57\t- **Motion detection (`signal/features.go`):**\n58\t  - NBVI (Normalized Bandwidth Variance Index): `Var(amp) / Mean(amp)²`\n59\t  - Welford's online algorithm for numerically stable variance\n60\t  - Top-16 subcarrier selection by NBVI score\n61\t  - deltaRMS: `sqrt(mean((amp - baseline)²))` over selected subcarriers\n62\t  - Exponential smoothing: `smooth = 0.3×raw + 0.7×prev`\n63\t  - Motion threshold: smoothDeltaRMS > 0.02\n64\t\n65\t- **Link processor (`signal/processor.go`):**\n66\t  - LinkProcessor: ties together phase sanitization, baseline, motion detection\n67\t  - ProcessorManager: manages per-link processors with thread-safe access\n68\t  - GetAllMotionStates(): returns motion state for all links\n69\t  - GetAllBaselines()/RestoreBaseline(): for SQLite persistence\n70\t\n71\t**Constants used (from plan):**\n72\t- RSSIRefdBm = -30.0\n73\t- DefaultMotionThreshold = 0.05\n74\t- DefaultDeltaRMSThreshold = 0.02\n75\t- NBVITopCount = 16\n76\t- NBVIMinThreshold = 0.001\n77\t- DeltaRMSSmoothingAlpha = 0.3\n78\t\n79\t**Tests:** 37 tests covering phase sanitization, baseline, NBVI, motion detection. Spaxel Implementation Progress\n\n## Phase 1 — Foundation\n\nGoal: Bare-minimum loop from ESP32 to browser. Zero-config with passive radar and mDNS from day one.\n\n### Status: COMPLETE\n\n| Item | Status | Notes |\n|------|--------|-------|\n| ESP32 firmware skeleton | **Done** | See iteration 2 below |\n| Passive radar support | **Done** | BSSID filter in csi.c |\n| BLE scanning | **Done** | Core 0 concurrent with WiFi |\n| Mothership WebSocket ingestion | **Done** | See iteration 1 below |\n| Dashboard skeleton | **Done** | See iteration 3 below |\n| Docker packaging | **Done** | See iteration 4 below |\n\n## Phase 2 — Signal Processing & Detection\n\nGoal: Detect presence on a single link.\n\n### Status\n\n| Item | Status | Notes |\n|------|--------|-------|\n| Phase sanitisation | **Done** | See iteration 5 below |\n| Baseline system | **Done** | EMA with motion-gated updates |\n| Motion detection | **Done** | deltaRMS, NBVI selection |\n| Dashboard presence indicator | **Pending** | |\n| CSI recording buffer | **Pending** | |\n| Adaptive sensing rate | **Pending** | |\n\n### Iteration Log\n\n#### Iteration 5 — 2026-03-26\n\n**Completed:** Signal processing package (phase sanitisation, baseline, motion detection)\n\nImplemented the core signal processing pipeline in Go with:\n\n- **Phase sanitization (`signal/phase.go`):**\n  - Complex CSI computation: int8 I/Q → float64 complex → amplitude/phase\n  - RSSI normalization (AGC compensation): `norm = 10^((rssi_ref - rssi)/20)`\n  - Spatial phase unwrapping: correct 2π jumps between adjacent subcarriers\n  - Linear regression (OLS): fit `phase = a*k + b` over data subcarriers\n  - STO/CFO removal: residual phase = unwrapped - (slope × k + intercept)\n  - HT20 subcarrier map: 64 total, 46 data (excluding null, guard, pilot)\n\n- **Baseline system (`signal/baseline.go`):**\n  - EMA baseline per link per subcarrier: `baseline = α×amplitude + (1-α)×baseline`\n  - α = dt / (τ + dt) ≈ 0.0033 for dt=0.1s, τ=30s\n  - Motion-gated updates: only update when smoothDeltaRMS < 0.05\n  - Confidence scoring: 0.3 for stale baselines (>7 days), asymptotically → 1.0\n  - Snapshot/restore for SQLite persistence\n  - BaselineManager for multi-link coordination\n\n- **Motion detection (`signal/features.go`):**\n  - NBVI (Normalized Bandwidth Variance Index): `Var(amp) / Mean(amp)²`\n  - Welford's online algorithm for numerically stable variance\n  - Top-16 subcarrier selection by NBVI score\n  - deltaRMS: `sqrt(mean((amp - baseline)²))` over selected subcarriers\n  - Exponential smoothing: `smooth = 0.3×raw + 0.7×prev`\n  - Motion threshold: smoothDeltaRMS > 0.02\n\n- **Link processor (`signal/processor.go`):**\n  - LinkProcessor: ties together phase sanitization, baseline, motion detection\n  - ProcessorManager: manages per-link processors with thread-safe access\n  - GetAllMotionStates(): returns motion state for all links\n  - GetAllBaselines()/RestoreBaseline(): for SQLite persistence\n\n**Constants used (from plan):**\n- RSSIRefdBm = -30.0\n- DefaultMotionThreshold = 0.05\n- DefaultDeltaRMSThreshold = 0.02\n- NBVITopCount = 16\n- NBVIMinThreshold = 0.001\n- DeltaRMSSmoothingAlpha = 0.3\n\n**Tests:** 37 tests covering phase sanitization, baseline, NBVI, motion detection. All pass.\n\n**Files created:**\n```\nmothership/internal/signal/\n├── phase.go          — Phase sanitization algorithms\n├── phase_test.go     — 15 tests for phase processing\n├── baseline.go       — EMA baseline management\n├── baseline_test.go  — 9 tests for baseline\n├── features.go       — NBVI selection, deltaRMS, motion detection\n├── features_test.go  — 13 tests for features\n└── processor.go      — LinkProcessor, ProcessorManager\n```\n\n**Remaining for Phase 2:**\n- Dashboard presence indicator\n- CSI recording buffer\n- Adaptive sensing rate\n\n#### Iteration 4 — 2026-03-26\n\n**Completed:** Docker packaging\n\nImplemented container deployment with:\n\n- **Dockerfile:** Multi-stage build for minimal production image\n  - Stage 1: `golang:1.23-bookworm` builder with module caching\n  - Stage 2: `distroless/static-debian12:nonroot` runtime (no shell, runs as UID 65532)\n  - CGO_ENABLED=0 build (pure-Go SQLite compatible)\n  - Dashboard static files copied to `/dashboard/`\n  - Firmware directory as volume mount point (`/firmware/`)\n  - Built-in healthcheck via wget\n\n- **docker-compose.yml:** Production-ready orchestration\n  - `network_mode: host` for mDNS multicast discovery (required for ESP32 nodes)\n  - Volume mounts: `spaxel-data` for persistence, `./firmware` for OTA binaries\n  - Environment variables: TZ, mDNS config, optional MQTT\n  - Resource limits: 512m RAM, 2 CPUs (scalable to 1g for 16+ nodes)\n  - 35s stop_grace_period for graceful shutdown\n  - Ulimits: 4096/8192 file descriptors for node connections\n  - Traefik labels (disabled by default, enable for TLS/proxy)\n\n- **Supporting files:**\n  - `VERSION` — 0.1.0 for image tagging\n  - `.dockerignore` — Excludes docs, build artifacts, IDE files\n\n**Key design decisions:**\n- Host networking is REQUIRED for mDNS — Docker bridge blocks multicast 224.0.0.251\n- distroless image minimizes attack surface (no shell, no package manager)\n- Firmware binaries not bundled — users mount their own `/firmware` volume\n- Traefik labels included but disabled — enable for production TLS\n\n**Files created:**\n```\nDockerfile\ndocker-compose.yml\nVERSION\n.dockerignore\n```\n\n**Phase 1 Status:** COMPLETE\n\nAll Phase 1 items implemented:\n- ✅ ESP32 firmware skeleton\n- ✅ Passive radar support\n- ✅ BLE scanning\n- ✅ Mothership WebSocket ingestion\n- ✅ Dashboard skeleton\n- ✅ Docker packaging\n\n**Next:** Phase 2 — Signal Processing (baseline, deltaRMS, Fresnel zones)\n\n#### Iteration 3 — 2026-03-26\n\n**Completed:** Dashboard skeleton with 3D visualization\n\nImplemented the web dashboard in vanilla JS + Three.js with:\n\n- **Static file structure:** `dashboard/` directory with HTML/JS\n  - `index.html` — Dark theme UI with status bar, node/link panels, amplitude chart\n  - `js/app.js` — Main application (~350 lines)\n\n- **3D Scene (Three.js):**\n  - Ground grid (10×10m, 20 divisions)\n  - Auto-reconnect with 3s backoff\n  - JSON message handling for state, node events, link events\n  - Binary CSI frame parsing (24-byte header + I/Q payload)\n  - Connection status indicator (green/red dot)\n\n- **Node/Link panels:**\n  - Real-time update from selected link's CSI frames\n  - Color gradient based on amplitude intensity\n  - Channel and RSSI display overlay\n\n- **Dashboard package (Go):**\n  - `internal/dashboard/hub.go` — Hub for client management and broadcasting\n  - `internal/dashboard/server.go` — WebSocket handler with ping/pong keepalive\n  - `internal/dashboard/hub_test.go` — 5 tests for hub operations\n  - IngestionState interface for querying node/link state\n  - CSIBroadcaster interface implementation\n\n- **Main.go updates:**\n  - Static file serving with SPA fallback\n  - Dashboard WebSocket endpoint at `/ws/dashboard`\n  - Auto-discovery of dashboard directory\n  - Wiring between ingestion and dashboard packages\n\n**Dependencies used (frontend):**\n- Three.js r128 (CDN)\n- OrbitControls (CDN)\n\n**Tests:** 5 new tests for dashboard hub. Top-level `CMakeLists.txt`, `sdkconfig.defaults`, `partitions.csv` (factory + OTA slots)\n  - `main/` component with 5 source modules\n\n- **State machine (main.c):** 7-state node lifecycle\n  - BOOT → WIFI_CONNECTING → MOTHERSHIP_DISCOVERY → CONNECTED\n  - Degraded states: WIFI_LOST, MOTHERSHIP_UNAVAILABLE, CAPTIVE_PORTAL\n  - Exponential backoff on WiFi failures (1s → 30s max)\n  - 10-failure threshold before captive portal\n\n- **WiFi module (wifi.c/h):**\n  - STA connection with exponential backoff\n  - mDNS discovery for `_spaxel._tcp.local` with fallback to cached IP\n  - Captive portal AP mode (`spaxel-XXXX`) with HTTP config page\n  - Event-driven connection state via FreeRTOS event group\n\n- **WebSocket client (websocket.c/h):**\n  - Bidirectional communication on single connection\n  - Binary CSI frame transmission (24-byte header + I/Q payload)\n  - JSON message handling: hello, health, ble, ota_status upstream\n  - Downstream command parsing: role, config, ota, reboot, identify, reject\n  - OTA download task with progress reporting and automatic reboot\n\n- **CSI capture (csi.c/h):**\n  - WiFi promiscuous mode with CSI callback\n  - Queue-based processing (32-frame buffer)\n  - Passive mode BSSID filtering for radar\n  - On-device amplitude variance tracking for motion hints (Welford's algorithm)\n  - TX task for active probing\n\n- **BLE scanner (ble.c/h):**\n  - Passive BLE scanning on Core 0 (concurrent with WiFi)\n  - Device cache (60 entries) with name and manufacturer data parsing\n  - 5-second reporting interval via WebSocket\n  - GAP event handling for advertisement processing\n\n- **NVS persistence:** Full schema with 15 keys\n  - WiFi credentials, node ID/token, mothership config\n  - Role/rate persistence for degraded mode operation\n  - Schema versioning for migration support\n\n**Files created:**\n```\nfirmware/\n├── CMakeLists.txt\n├── sdkconfig.defaults\n├── partitions.csv\n└── main/\n    ├── CMakeLists.txt\n    ├── spaxel.h\n    ├── main.c\n    ├── wifi.h / wifi.c\n    ├── websocket.h / websocket.c\n    ├── csi.h / csi.c\n    └── ble.h / ble.c\n```\n\n**Phase 1 Status:** COMPLETE\n\nAll Phase 1 items implemented:\n- ✅ ESP32 firmware skeleton\n- ✅ Passive radar support\n- ✅ BLE scanning\n- ✅ Mothership WebSocket ingestion\n- ✅ Dashboard skeleton\n- ✅ Docker packaging\n\n**Next:** Phase 2 — Signal Processing (baseline, deltaRMS, Fresnel zones)\n\n#### Iteration 1 — 2026-03-26\n\n**Completed:** Mothership WebSocket ingestion server\n\nImplemented the core ingestion server in Go with:\n\n- **Module structure:** `mothership/` with `cmd/mothership/` entrypoint and `internal/ingestion/` package\n- **WebSocket endpoint:** `/ws/node` accepts bidirectional connections from ESP32 nodes\n- **Binary frame parsing:** 24-byte header + variable payload, per spec in plan.md\n  - Validation: min/max length, payload size match, channel validity (1-14), subcarrier limit (128)\n  - Malformed frame tracking with warn/close thresholds (100/1000 per minute)\n- **JSON message handling:** Parses hello, health, ble, motion_hint, ota_status\n- **Per-link ring buffers:** 256-sample circular buffers keyed by `nodeMAC:peerMAC`\n- **Connection lifecycle:** Node registration via hello, ping/pong keepalive (30s/60s), graceful shutdown\n- **mDNS advertisement:** `_spaxel._tcp.local` via github.com/hashicorp/mdns\n- **Role/config push:** Sends initial `rx` role and 20 Hz config on connect\n- **Health endpoint:** `GET /healthz` returns `{\"status\":\"ok\",\"version\":\"...\"}`\n\n**Dependencies used:**\n- `github.com/go-chi/chi` — HTTP routing\n- `github.com/gorilla/websocket` — WebSocket server\n- `github.com/hashicorp/mdns` — mDNS advertisement\n\n**Tests:** 22 tests covering frame parsing, JSON messages, and ring buffer operations. tracking (`prediction/model.go`):**\n  - `zone_transitions_history` table with all zone transitions (person_id, from_zone, to_zone, hour_of_week, dwell_duration)\n  - `transition_probabilities` table with Laplace-smoothed probabilities\n  - `dwell_times` table with mean/stddev dwell time per person/zone/hour\n  - `person_zone_entry` table for tracking current person positions\n  - Zone transition recording via `PersonZoneChange()` method\n  - Automatic probability recomputation with Laplace smoothing\n  - Dwell time statistics with mean/stddev computation\n\n- **Per-zone occupancy patterns (`prediction/accuracy.go`):**\n  - `zone_occupancy_patterns` table with occupancy_prob per zone/hour_of_week\n  - `zone_occupancy_history` table tracking entries/exits with timestamps\n  - `recorded_predictions` table for tracking prediction accuracy\n  - `accuracy_stats` table with rolling 7-day accuracy metrics\n  - Zone occupancy pattern computation from historical data\n  - Pattern-based occupancy prediction at target time\n\n- **Time-slot based predictions (`prediction/horizon.go`):**\n  - Monte Carlo simulation with 1000 runs for probabilistic predictions\n  - Multi-step path simulation accounting for dwell times\n  - Normal distribution sampling for dwell time variability\n  - Horizon predictions at 5, 15, and 30 minutes\n  - Returns probability distribution over all zones\n  - Confidence scoring based on simulation agreement\n\n- **HA sensor exposure (`mqtt/client.go`):**\n  - `PublishPredictionSensors()` creates HA auto-discovery configs\n  - `UpdatePredictionState()` publishes current predictions to MQTT\n  - Three sensors per person:\n    - `sensor.spaxel_<person>_predicted_zone` - zone name\n    - `sensor.spaxel_<person>_prediction_confidence` - percentage\n    - `sensor.spaxel_<person>_transition_minutes` - estimated minutes\n  - Topics follow HA discovery pattern: `homeassistant/sensor/.../config`\n\n- **REST API endpoints (`api/prediction.go`):**\n  - `GET /api/predictions` - Get current predictions for all people\n  - `GET /api/predictions?person=<id>&horizon=<min>` - Filtered predictions\n  - `GET /api/predictions/stats` - Transition count, data age, model readiness\n  - `POST /api/predictions/recompute` - Force probability recomputation\n  - `GET /api/predictions/accuracy` - Per-person accuracy stats\n  - `GET /api/predictions/accuracy/overall` - Overall system accuracy\n  - `GET /api/predictions/accuracy/{personID}` - Person-specific accuracy\n  - `GET /api/predictions/horizon` - Monte Carlo horizon predictions\n  - `GET /api/predictions/horizon/{personID}` - Person-specific horizon prediction\n  - `GET /api/predictions/patterns/zones` - Zone occupancy patterns\n  - `GET /api/predictions/probabilities/{personID}` - Transition probabilities\n  - `GET /api/predictions/samples/{personID}/zone/{zoneID}` - Sample counts\n\n- **Main application wiring (`cmd/mothership/main.go`):**\n  - Prediction module initialization (lines 492-534)\n  - Zone transition recording on portal crossings (lines 1579-1581)\n  - Provider wiring for zones, people, positions (lines 1831-1863)\n  - MQTT client integration for prediction publishing (lines 1846-1863)\n  - Periodic prediction update loop every 60 seconds (lines 1866-1888)\n  - Periodic prediction evaluation every 30 seconds (lines 1931-1983)\n  - REST API endpoint registration (lines 2527-2888)\n\n**Constants and thresholds:**\n- MinimumDataAge = 7 days (168 hours) before predictions activate\n- MinimumSamplesPerSlot = 3 observations per time slot\n- PredictionHorizon = 15 minutes (default)\n- MonteCarloRuns = 1000 simulations\n- TargetAccuracy = 75% at 15-minute horizon\n\n**Accuracy tracking:**\n- Records predictions when made (personID, currentZone, predictedZone, confidence, horizon)\n- Evaluates pending predictions when target time is reached\n- Compares predicted zone vs actual zone\n- Computes rolling 7-day accuracy percentage\n- Reports \"meets_target\" when accuracy ≥ 75% and min predictions threshold met\n\n**Model learning:**\n- Observations recorded every 5 minutes per person/zone\n- EMA update: `p_new = p_old + α × (obs - p_old)` where α = 0.03\n- Cold start: 7 days of data required for model readiness\n- Slot ready when sample_count ≥ 3\n- Automatic recomputation triggered on zone transitions\n\n**Files buffered queue\n- Handles all event types: detections, zone transitions, alerts, system events, learning milestones\n- WebSocket push of new events to dashboard clients in real-time\n- REST API: `GET \"Apply to Live\" copies tuned params to the running pipeline\n- Dashboard: timeline scrubber integrated with activity timeline events\n\n**Pre-deployment simulator (`internal/simulator/`):**\n- `Space` defines room geometry with wall segments and material properties\n- `NodeSet` manages virtual TX/RX node placement\n- `WalkerSet` simulates random-walk or path-following persons\n- Two-ray propagation model with path loss + wall penetration + first-order reflections\n- GDOP computation per cell using Fisher information matrix\n- REST API at `/api/simulator/*` for space/node/walker management and GDOP computation\n\n**CSI simulator CLI (`cmd/sim/`):**\n- Connects to mothership as virtual nodes, sends synthetic CSI binary frames\n- Walker random-walk model with Gaussian velocity updates, wall reflection\n- Amplitude/phase generated from propagation model with Gaussian noise injection\n- Optional BLE advertisement simulation (`--ble` flag)\n- Verified authentication (exits non-zero on `{type:\"reject\"}`)\n- Integration test support: polls `GET /api/blobs` for blob count assertions\n\n**Fresnel zone debug overlay (`dashboard/js/fresnel.js`):**\n- Toggle-able wireframe ellipsoids between active TX/RX link pairs\n- Ellipsoid geometry computed from TX/RX positions and Fresnel zone number\n- Color-coded by zone number (zone 1 = bright, outer zones = dimmer)\n- Toolbar button integration with existing layers panel\n\n**Files created/modified:**\n```\nmothership/internal/timeline/       — timeline.go, timeline_test.go, handler.go, expert mode only\n\n**Morning briefing (`internal/briefing/`, `dashboard/js/briefing.js`):**\n- `Generator` assembles briefing in priority order from sleep, events, anomalies, system health\n- Priority blocks: critical alerts → sleep summary → who is home → overnight anomalies → system health → predictions → learning progress\n- Degenerate case: \"All quiet last night. dismissible, slides away after 10 s\n\n**Guided troubleshooting (`internal/guidedtroubleshoot/`, `dashboard/js/troubleshoot.js`):**\n- Trigger conditions: detection quality drops, repeated setting changes, node offline >2 h\n- Repeated-edit detection: per-key counter in memory; hint delivered in `GET /api/settings` response as `\"repeated_edit_hint\": true`\n- Proactive diagnostic flow: check connectivity → show link health → suggest repositioning → offer re-baseline\n- First-time feature tooltips shown once, dismissed on click, stored in `localStorage`\n- Post-feedback explanations in timeline after incorrect/missed detection feedback\n\n**Mobile-responsive expert mode (`dashboard/js/mobile.js` + CSS):**\n- Touch orbit (one finger), pan (two fingers), pinch-zoom (two fingers)\n- layout\n- CSS Grid layout collapses gracefully on narrow viewports\n\n**Fleet status page (`dashboard/fleet.html`, `dashboard/js/fleet-page.js`):**\n- Full table with sortable columns: Name, MAC, * Spaxel First-Time Feature Tooltips\n *\n * Shows contextual tooltips on first dashboard open after a node is added.\n * Each tooltip is shown once and tracked via localStorage flags.\n *\n * TooltipManager.show(tooltipId, targetSelector, text, direction)\n * TooltipManager.showSequence() — walks through the manifest automatically\n */\n\n(function () {\n    'use strict';\n\n    //
