// auto-dismiss after 8 s\n    var SEQUENCE_GAP_MS = 2000;   // pause between sequential tooltips\n\n    var TOOLTIP_MANIFEST = [\n        {\n            id: 'csi-chart',\n            target: '#chart-panel',\n            text: 'This is your live signal. Motion causes the waves to change.',\n            direction: 'left',\n        },\n        {\n            id: '3d-view',\n            target: '#scene-container',\n            text: 'This 3D space updates as people move around.',\n            direction: 'top',\n        },\n        {\n            id: 'presence-indicator',\n            target: '#presence-indicator',\n            text: 'Green = no one detected. Red = motion detected.',\n            direction: 'bottom',\n        },\n        {\n            id: 'link-list',\n            target: '.link-section',\n            text: 'Each line between two nodes is a sensing link.',\n            direction: 'left',\n        },\n    ];\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Internal State\n    // ============================================\n    var state = {\n        activeTooltip: null,\n        dismissTimer: null,\n        sequenceTimer: null,\n        sequenceIndex: 0,\n    };\n\n    // ============================================\n    // localStorage Helpers\n    // ============================================\n    function hasShown(id) {\n        try {\n            return localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_PREFIX + id + '_shown') === 'true';\n        } catch (e) {\n            return false;\n        }\n    }\n\n    function markShown(id) {\n        try {\n            localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_PREFIX + id + '_shown', 'true');\n        } catch (e) { /* ignore */ }\n    }\n\n    function shouldShowSequence() {\n        try {\n            return localStorage.getItem('spaxel_tooltips_shown') !== 'true';\n        } catch (e) {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    function markAllShown() {\n        try {\n            localStorage.setItem('spaxel_tooltips_shown', 'true');\n        } catch (e) { /* ignore */ }\n    }\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Tooltip Rendering\n    // ============================================\n    function show(tooltipId, targetSelector, text, direction) {\n        if (hasShown(tooltipId)) return false;\n\n        dismiss();\n\n        var target = document.querySelector(targetSelector);\n        if (!target) return false;\n\n        var tooltip = document.createElement('div');\n        tooltip.className = 'spaxel-tooltip';\n        tooltip.id = 'spaxel-tooltip-' + tooltipId;\n        tooltip.innerHTML =\n            '<div class=\"spaxel-tooltip-text\">' + escapeHTML(text) + '</div>' +\n            '<div class=\"spaxel-tooltip-arrow spaxel-tooltip-arrow-' + (direction || 'top') + '\"></div>';\n\n        // Append first so we can measure dimensions\n        tooltip.style.visibility = 'hidden';\n        document.body.appendChild(tooltip);\n\n        var rect = target.getBoundingClientRect();\n        var tipRect = tooltip.getBoundingClientRect();\n        var top, left, transform;\n\n        switch (direction) {\n            case 'bottom':\n                top = rect.bottom + 10;\n                left = rect.left + rect.width / 2 - tipRect.width / 2;\n                transform = '';\n                break;\n            case 'left':\n                top = rect.top + rect.height / 2 - tipRect.height / 2;\n                left = rect.left - tipRect.width - 10;\n                transform = '';\n                break;\n            case 'right':\n                top = rect.top + rect.height / 2 - tipRect.height / 2;\n                left = rect.right + 10;\n                transform = '';\n                break;\n            default: // top\n                top = rect.top - tipRect.height - 10;\n                left = rect.left + rect.width / 2 - tipRect.width / 2;\n                transform = '';\n                break;\n        }\n\n        // Clamp to viewport\n        var vpW = window.innerWidth;\n        var vpH = window.innerHeight;\n        left = Math.max(8, Math.min(left, vpW - tipRect.width - 8));\n        top = Math.max(8, Math.min(top, vpH - tipRect.height - 8));\n\n        tooltip.style.top = top + 'px';\n        tooltip.style.left = left + 'px';\n        tooltip.style.transform = transform;\n        tooltip.style.visibility = 'visible';\n\n        state.activeTooltip = tooltip;\n\n        // Auto-dismiss\n        state.dismissTimer = setTimeout(function () {\n            markShown(tooltipId);\n            dismiss();\n        }, DISMISS_MS);\n\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    function dismiss() {\n        if (state.dismissTimer) {\n            clearTimeout(state.dismissTimer);\n            state.dismissTimer = null;\n        }\n        if (state.activeTooltip) {\n            if (state.activeTooltip.parentNode) {\n                state.activeTooltip.parentNode.removeChild(state.activeTooltip);\n            }\n            state.activeTooltip = null;\n        }\n    }\n\n    function dismissAll() {\n        dismiss();\n        if (state.sequenceTimer) {\n            clearTimeout(state.sequenceTimer);\n            state.sequenceTimer = null;\n        }\n        markAllShown();\n        hideDismissAllButton();\n    }\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Sequential Tooltip Tour\n    // ============================================\n    function showSequence() {\n        if (!shouldShowSequence()) return;\n\n        // Collect tooltips whose targets exist in the DOM\n        var visible = [];\n        for (var i = 0; i < TOOLTIP_MANIFEST.length; i++) {\n            var t = TOOLTIP_MANIFEST[i];\n            if (!hasShown(t.id) && document.querySelector(t.target)) {\n                visible.push(t);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (visible.length === 0) {\n            markAllShown();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        showDismissAllButton();\n\n        // Show first tooltip\n        if (show(visible[0].id, visible[0].target, visible[0].text, visible[0].direction)) {\n            state.sequenceIndex = 1;\n            scheduleNext(visible);\n        } else {\n            markAllShown();\n            hideDismissAllButton();\n        }\n    }\n\n    function scheduleNext(visible) {\n        if (state.sequenceIndex >= visible.length) {\n            markAllShown();\n            hideDismissAllButton();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        state.sequenceTimer = setTimeout(function () {\n            var next = visible[state.sequenceIndex];\n            if (show(next.id, next.target, next.text, next.direction)) {\n                state.sequenceIndex++;\n                scheduleNext(visible);\n            } else {\n                // Target no longer visible — skip\n                state.sequenceIndex++;\n                scheduleNext(visible);\n            }\n        }, DISMISS_MS + SEQUENCE_GAP_MS);\n    }\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Dismiss-All Button\n    // ============================================\n    function showDismissAllButton() {\n        if (document.getElementById('spaxel-dismiss-all-tooltips')) return;\n\n        var btn = document.createElement('button');\n        btn.id = 'spaxel-dismiss-all-tooltips';\n        btn.className = 'spaxel-dismiss-all';\n        btn.textContent = 'Dismiss all tips';\n        btn.addEventListener('click', dismissAll);\n        document.body.appendChild(btn);\n    }\n\n    function hideDismissAllButton() {\n        var btn = document.getElementById('spaxel-dismiss-all-tooltips');\n        if (btn && btn.parentNode) btn.parentNode.removeChild(btn);\n    }\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Helpers\n    // ============================================\n    function escapeHTML(s) {\n        var div = document.createElement('div');\n        div.textContent = s;\n        return div.innerHTML;\n    }\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Feature Panel Tooltips\n    // ============================================\n    var FEATURE_PANEL_TOOLTIPS = {\n        'settings-panel': {\n            id: 'settings-intro',\n            target: '#settings-panel',\n            text: 'Adjust sensitivity, fusion rate, and detection thresholds. Changes take effect immediately.',\n            direction: 'left',\n        },\n        'automation-panel': {\n            id: 'automation-intro',\n            target: '#automation-panel',\n            text: 'Create triggers based on presence, dwell time, or motion. Automate your space.',\n            direction: 'left',\n        },\n        'replay-panel': {\n            id: 'replay-intro',\n            target: '#replay-panel',\n            text: 'Replay CSI data from the past 48 hours. Great for debugging and algorithm tuning.',\n            direction: 'left',\n        },\n        'linkhealth-panel': {\n            id: 'linkhealth-intro',\n            target: '#linkhealth-panel',\n            text: 'Monitor link quality and signal strength. Identify weak links affecting detection.',\n            direction: 'left',\n        },\n    };\n\n    var FEATURE_PANEL_PREFIX = 'spaxel_feature_panel_';\n\n    function hasShownFeaturePanel(panelId) {\n        try {\n            return localStorage.getItem(FEATURE_PANEL_PREFIX + panelId + '_shown') === 'true';\n        } catch (e) {\n            return false;\n        }\n    }\n\n    function markFeaturePanelShown(panelId) {\n        try {\n            localStorage.setItem(FEATURE_PANEL_PREFIX + panelId + '_shown', 'true');\n        } catch (e) { /* ignore */ }\n    }\n\n    function showFeatureTooltip(panelId) {\n        var tooltipConfig = FEATURE_PANEL_TOOLTIPS[panelId];\n        if (!tooltipConfig) return false;\n\n        if (hasShownFeaturePanel(panelId)) return false;\n\n        var target = document.querySelector(tooltipConfig.target);\n        if (!target) return false;\n\n        var tooltip = document.createElement('div');\n        tooltip.className = 'spaxel-tooltip spaxel-feature-tooltip';\n        tooltip.id = 'spaxel-tooltip-' + tooltipConfig.id;\n        tooltip.innerHTML =\n            '<div class=\"spaxel-tooltip-text\">' + escapeHTML(tooltipConfig.text) + '</div>' +\n            '<div class=\"spaxel-tooltip-close\" onclick=\"SpaxelTooltips.dismissFeatureTooltip(\\'' + panelId + '\\')\">✕</div>' +\n            '<div class=\"spaxel-tooltip-arrow spaxel-tooltip-arrow-' + (tooltipConfig.direction || 'top') + '\"></div>';\n\n        // Append first so we can measure dimensions\n        tooltip.style.visibility = 'hidden';\n        document.body.appendChild(tooltip);\n\n        var rect = target.getBoundingClientRect();\n        var tipRect = tooltip.getBoundingClientRect();\n        var top, left, transform;\n\n        switch (tooltipConfig.direction) {\n            case 'bottom':\n                top = rect.bottom + 10;\n                left = rect.left + rect.width / 2 - tipRect.width / 2;\n                transform = '';\n                break;\n            case 'left':\n                top = rect.top + rect.height / 2 - tipRect.height / 2;\n                left = rect.left - tipRect.width - 10;\n                transform = '';\n                break;\n            case 'right':\n                top = rect.top + rect.height / 2 - tipRect.height / 2;\n                left = rect.right + 10;\n                transform = '';\n                break;\n            default: // top\n                top = rect.top - tipRect.height - 10;\n                left = rect.left + rect.width / 2 - tipRect.width / 2;\n                transform = '';\n                break;\n        }\n\n        // Clamp to viewport\n        var vpW = window.innerWidth;\n        var vpH = window.innerHeight;\n        left = Math.max(8, Math.min(left, vpW - tipRect.width - 8));\n        top = Math.max(8, Math.min(top, vpH - tipRect.height - 8));\n\n        tooltip.style.top = top + 'px';\n        tooltip.style.left = left + 'px';\n        tooltip.style.transform = transform;\n        tooltip.style.visibility = 'visible';\n\n        state.featureTooltip = tooltip;\n\n        // Auto-dismiss after longer duration for feature panels\n        setTimeout(function () {\n            dismissFeatureTooltip(panelId);\n        }, 12000); // 12 seconds instead of 8\n\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    function dismissFeatureTooltip(panelId) {\n        if (state.featureTooltip) {\n            markFeaturePanelShown(panelId);\n            if (state.featureTooltip.parentNode) {\n                state.featureTooltip.parentNode.removeChild(state.featureTooltip);\n            }\n            state.featureTooltip = null;\n        }\n    }\n\n    function onFeaturePanelOpen(panelId) {\n        // Delay slightly to allow panel to render\n        setTimeout(function () {\n            showFeatureTooltip(panelId);\n        }, 300);\n    }\n\n    // ============================================\n    // Public API\n    // ============================================\n    window.SpaxelTooltips = {\n        show: show,\n        dismiss: dismiss,\n        dismissAll: dismissAll,\n        showSequence: showSequence,\n        showFeatureTooltip: showFeatureTooltip,\n        dismissFeatureTooltip: dismissFeatureTooltip,\n        onFeaturePanelOpen: onFeaturePanelOpen,\n        // Exposed for testing\n        _TOOLTIP_MANIFEST: TOOLTIP_MANIFEST,\n        _FEATURE_PANEL_TOOLTIPS: FEATURE_PANEL_TOOLTIPS,\n        _STORAGE_PREFIX: STORAGE_PREFIX,\n        _DISMISS_MS: DISMISS_MS,\n        _state: state,\n    };\n})();\n","numLines":414,"startLine":1,"totalLines":414}}}

//nolint:errcheck\n100\t\n101\t\t// Clear existing cache\n102\t\ts.cache = make(map[string]interface{})\n103\t\n104\t\tfor rows.Next() {\n105\t\t\tvar key, valueJSON string\n106\t\t\tif err := returns all settings as a map.\n125\t// Default values are included for keys that don't exist in the database.\n126\tfunc (s *SettingsHandler) Get() map[string]interface{} {\n127\t\ts.mu.RLock()\n128\t\tdefer s.mu.RUnlock()\n129\t\n130\t\tresult := make(map[string]interface{}, len(s.cache)+len(defaultSettings))\n131\t\n132\t\t// First add cached values\n133\t\tfor k, v false\n163\t}\n164\t\n165\t// Set updates a single setting value.\n166\t// The value is marshaled to JSON and stored in the database.\n167\tfunc (s *SettingsHandler) Set(key string, value interface{}) caller must hold s.mu.\n176\tfunc (s *SettingsHandler) setLocked(key string, value interface{}) error {\n177\t\tvalueJSON, err := json.Marshal(value)\n178\t\tif err != nil {\n179\t\t\treturn err\n180\t\t}\n181\t\n182\t\tnow := time.Now().UnixMilli()\n183\t\t_, err = s.db.Exec(`\n184\t\t\tINSERT INTO settings (key, value_json, updated_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\n185\t\t\tON CONFLICT(key) DO UPDATE SET value_json = ?, updated_at = ?\n186\t\t`, key, string(valueJSON), now, string(valueJSON), now)\n187\t\tif err != nil {\n188\t\t\treturn err\n189\t\t}\n190\t\n191\t\ts.cache[key] = value\n192\t\treturn nil\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// Update merges a partial settings map with existing settings.\n196\t// Only the keys provided in the updates map are modified.\n197\tfunc (s *SettingsHandler) Update(updates map[string]interface{}) error {\n198\t\ts.mu.Lock()\n199\t\tdefer s.mu.Unlock()\n200\t\n201\t\tfor key, value := range updates {\n202\t\t\tif err := s.setLocked(key, value); err != nil {\n203\t\t\t\treturn err\n204\t\t\t}\n205\t\t}\n206\t\treturn nil\n207\t}\n208\t\n209\t// Delete removes a setting from both the database and cache.\n210\tfunc (s *SettingsHandler) Delete(key string) error {\n211\t\ts.mu.Lock()\n212\t\tdefer s.mu.Unlock()\n213\t\n214\t\t_, err := s.db.Exec(`DELETE FROM settings WHERE key = ?`, key)\n215\t\tif err != nil {\n216\t\t\treturn hasn't been set in the database.\n225\tvar defaultSettings = map[string]interface{}{\n226\t\t\"fusion_rate_hz\":        10.0,   // Fusion loop rate in Hz\n227\t\t\"grid_cell_m\":           0.2,    // (0-1)\n250\t}\n251\t\n252\t// RegisterRoutes registers settings endpoints on the given router.\n253\t//\n254\t// Settings Endpoints:\n255\t//\n256\t//\tGET  /api/settings — Return all configurable Package api provides REST API handlers for Spaxel settings.\npackage api\n\nimport (\n\t\"database/sql\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5\"\n\t_ \"modernc.org/sqlite\"\n)\n\n// SettingsHandler manages application settings.\n// Settings are stored as key-value pairs in the settings table with JSON-encoded values.\ntype SettingsHandler struct {\n\tmu   sync.RWMutex\n\tdb   *sql.DB\n\t// cache is an in-memory cache of settings for fast reads\n\tcache map[string]interface{}\n\t// editTracker tracks repeated edits for troubleshooting hints\n\teditTracker interface {\n\t\tRecordEdit(key string) (bool, bool)\n\t\tMarkHintShown(key string)\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSettingsHandler creates a new settings handler using the provided database connection.\n// The database connection must be to the main spaxel.db which contains the settings table.\nfunc NewSettingsHandler(db *sql.DB) *SettingsHandler {\n\ts := &SettingsHandler{\n\t\tdb:    db,\n\t\tcache: make(map[string]interface{}),\n\t}\n\t// Load initial settings into cache\n\tif err := s.load(); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to load settings: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// SetEditTracker sets the edit tracker for monitoring repeated settings changes.\nfunc (s *SettingsHandler) SetEditTracker(tracker interface {\n\tRecordEdit(key string) (bool, bool)\n\tMarkHintShown(key string)\n}) {\n\ts.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer s.mu.Unlock()\n\ts.editTracker = tracker\n}\n\n// NewSettingsHandlerWithPath creates a new settings handler by opening a database\n// at the specified path. This is a convenience function for handlers that manage\n// their own database connections.\nfunc NewSettingsHandlerWithPath(dbPath string) (*SettingsHandler, error) {\n\tdb, err := sql.Open(\"sqlite\", dbPath+\"?_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)&_pragma=synchronous(NORMAL)&_pragma=foreign_keys(ON)\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tdb.SetMaxOpenConns(1)\n\n\t// Verify //nolint:errcheck\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\ts := &SettingsHandler{\n\t\tdb:    db,\n\t\tcache: make(map[string]interface{}),\n\t}\n\t// Load initial settings into cache\n\tif err := s.load(); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to load settings: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\n// Close closes the database connection.\nfunc (s *SettingsHandler) Close() error {\n\treturn s.db.Close()\n}\n\n// load reads all settings from the database into the in-memory cache.\n// It must be called with the mutex lock held.\nfunc (s *SettingsHandler) load() error {\n\ts.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer s.mu.Unlock()\n\n\trows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT key, value_json FROM settings`)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tdefer rows.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n\n\t// Clear existing cache\n\ts.cache = make(map[string]interface{})\n\n\tfor rows.Next() {\n\t\tvar key, valueJSON string\n\t\tif err := rows.Scan(&key, &valueJSON); err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to scan setting key=%s: %v\", key, err)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar value interface{}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(valueJSON), &value); err != nil {\n\t\t\t// If not valid JSON, store as string\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Failed to unmarshal setting key=%s: %v\", key, err)\n\t\t\ts.cache[key] = valueJSON\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\ts.cache[key] = value\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn rows.Err()\n}\n\n// Get returns all settings as a map.\n// Default values are included for keys that don't exist in the database.\nfunc (s *SettingsHandler) Get() map[string]interface{} {\n\ts.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer s.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tresult := make(map[string]interface{}, len(s.cache)+len(defaultSettings))\n\n\t// First add cached values\n\tfor k, v := range s.cache {\n\t\tresult[k] = v\n\t}\n\n\t// Then add defaults for any missing keys\n\tfor k, v := range defaultSettings {\n\t\tif _, exists := s.cache[k]; !exists {\n\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// GetSingle returns a single setting value by key.\n// Returns the value, true if found, or nil, false if not found.\nfunc (s *SettingsHandler) GetSingle(key string) (interface{}, bool) {\n\ts.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer s.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif val, exists := s.cache[key]; exists {\n\t\treturn val, true\n\t}\n\n\t// Check defaults\n\tif val, exists := defaultSettings[key]; exists {\n\t\treturn val, true\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil, false\n}\n\n// Set updates a single setting value.\n// The value is marshaled to JSON and stored in the database.\nfunc (s *SettingsHandler) Set(key string, value interface{}) error {\n\ts.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer s.mu.Unlock()\n\n\treturn s.setLocked(key, value)\n}\n\n// setLocked updates a setting without acquiring the mutex.\n// The caller must hold s.mu.\nfunc (s *SettingsHandler) setLocked(key string, value interface{}) error {\n\tvalueJSON, err := json.Marshal(value)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tnow := time.Now().UnixMilli()\n\t_, err = s.db.Exec(`\n\t\tINSERT INTO settings (key, value_json, updated_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\n\t\tON CONFLICT(key) DO UPDATE SET value_json = ?, updated_at = ?\n\t`, key, string(valueJSON), now, string(valueJSON), now)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\ts.cache[key] = value\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Update merges a partial settings map with existing settings.\n// Only the keys provided in the updates map are modified.\nfunc (s *SettingsHandler) Update(updates map[string]interface{}) error {\n\ts.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer s.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tfor key, value := range updates {\n\t\tif err := s.setLocked(key, value); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Delete removes a setting from both the database and cache.\nfunc (s *SettingsHandler) Delete(key string) error {\n\ts.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer s.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t_, err := s.db.Exec(`DELETE FROM settings WHERE key = ?`, key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tdelete(s.cache, key)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// defaultSettings defines the default values for all known settings.\n// These are returned when a key hasn't been set in the database.\nvar defaultSettings = map[string]interface{}{\n\t\"fusion_rate_hz\":        10.0,   // Fusion loop rate in Hz\n\t\"grid_cell_m\":           0.2,    // Fresnel grid cell size in meters\n\t\"delta_rms_threshold\":   0.02,   // Motion detection threshold\n\t\"tau_s\":                 30.0,   // EMA baseline time constant in seconds\n\t\"fresnel_decay\":         2.0,    // Fresnel zone weight decay rate\n\t\"n_subcarriers\":         16,     // Number of subcarriers for NBVI selection\n\t\"breathing_sensitivity\": 0.005,  // Breathing detection threshold (radians RMS)\n\t\"motion_threshold\":      0.05,   // Smooth deltaRMS threshold for motion gating\n\t\"dwell_seconds\":         30,     // Default dwell trigger duration in seconds\n\t\"vacant_seconds\":        300,    // Default vacant trigger duration in seconds\n\t\"max_tracked_blobs\":     20,     // Maximum number of blobs to track simultaneously\n\t\"replay_retention_hours\": 48,     // CSI replay nil,    // Timestamp when security mode was armed\n\t\"events_archive_days\":   90,     // Events archive retention in days\n\t\"quiet_hours_start\":     \"\",     // Quiet hours start time (HH:MM format)\n\t\"quiet_hours_end\":       \"\",     // Quiet hours end time (HH:MM format)\n\t// Auto-update settings\n\t\"auto_update_enabled\":           false, // Auto-update mode enabled\n\t\"quiet_window_start\":           \"02:00\", // Auto-update quiet window start (HH:MM)\n\t\"quiet_window_end\":             \"05:00\", // Auto-update quiet window end (HH:MM)\n\t\"canary_duration_min\":          10,     // Canary monitoring duration in minutes\n\t\"auto_update_quality_threshold\": 0.05,  // Quality degradation threshold (0-1)\n}\n\n// RegisterRoutes registers settings endpoints on the given router.\n//\n// Settings Endpoints:\n//\n//\tGET  /api/settings — Return all configurable settings as JSON\n//\n//\t@Summary\t\tGet all settings\n//\t@Description\tReturns all system settings as a JSON object. Default values are included\n//\t@Description\tfor any settings that haven't been explicitly set.\n//\t@Tags\t\t\tsettings\n//\t@Produce\t\tjson\n//\t@Success\t\t200\t{object}\tmap[string]interface{}\t\"Settings object\"\n//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/settings [get]\n//\n//\tPOST /api/settings — Update settings (partial update, merge semantics)\n//\n//\t@Summary\t\tUpdate settings\n//\t@Description\tUpdates one or more settings. Only the keys provided in the request body\n//\t@Description\tare modified; other settings remain unchanged.\n//\t@Tags\t\t\tsettings\n//\t@Accept\t\t\tjson\n//\t@Produce\t\tjson\n//\t@Param\t\t\trequest\tbody\tmap[string]interface{}\ttrue\t\"Settings to update (partial)\"\n//\t@Success\t\t200\t{object}\tmap[string]interface{}\t\"Updated settings object\"\n//\t@Failure\t\t400\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Invalid request body or validation error\"\n//\t@Failure\t\t500\t{object}\tmap[string]string\t\"Failed to update settings\"\n//\t@Router\t\t\t/api/settings [post]\n//\n//\tPATCH /api/settings — Update settings (alias for POST)\nfunc (s *SettingsHandler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) {\n\tr.Get(\"/api/settings\", s.handleGetSettings)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/settings\", s.handleUpdateSettings)\n\tr.Patch(\"/api/settings\", s.handleUpdateSettings)\n}\n\n// handleGetSettings handles GET /api/settings requests.\nfunc (s *SettingsHandler) handleGetSettings(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tsettings := s.Get()\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, settings)\n}\n\n// handleUpdateSettings handles POST/PATCH /api/settings requests.\n// It validates known settings and applies partial updates with merge semantics.\nfunc (s *SettingsHandler) handleUpdateSettings(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar updates map[string]interface{}\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&updates); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate known settings\n\tif err := validateSettings(updates); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Track edits for troubleshooting hints\n\tvar hintPending bool\n\ts.mu.RLock()\n\ttracker := s.editTracker\n\ts.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif tracker != nil {\n\t\tfor key := range updates {\n\t\t\tif pending, _ := tracker.RecordEdit(key); pending {\n\t\t\t\thintPending = true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif err := s.Update(updates); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Failed to update settings: %v\", err)\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": \"failed to update settings\"})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get updated settings\n\tsettings := s.Get()\n\n\t// Add hint flag if pending\n\tif hintPending {\n\t\t// Consume the hint (mark as shown) - client-side will handle cooldown\n\t\tfor key := range updates {\n\t\t\ttracker.MarkHintShown(key)\n\t\t}\n\t\tsettings[\"repeated_edit_hint\"] = true\n\t}\n\n\t// Return updated settings\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, settings)\n}\n\n// validateSettings validates the provided settings values.\n// Returns an error if any setting value is outside its valid range.\nfunc validateSettings(settings map[string]interface{}) error {\n\t// Validate fusion_rate_hz: 1-20 Hz\n\tif v, ok := settings[\"fusion_rate_hz\"]; ok {\n\t\tif f, ok := asFloat64(v); !ok || f < 1 || f > 20 {\n\t\t\treturn &ValidationError{Key: \"fusion_rate_hz\", Reason: \"must be between 1 and 20\"}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate grid_cell_m: 0.05-1.0 meters\n\tif v, ok := settings[\"grid_cell_m\"]; ok {\n\t\tif f, ok := asFloat64(v); !ok || f < 0.05 || f > 1.0 {\n\t\t\treturn &ValidationError{Key: \"grid_cell_m\", Reason: \"must be between 0.05 and 1.0\"}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate delta_rms_threshold: 0.001-1.0\n\tif v, ok := settings[\"delta_rms_threshold\"]; ok {\n\t\tif f, ok := asFloat64(v); !ok || f < 0.001 || f > 1.0 {\n\t\t\treturn &ValidationError{Key: \"delta_rms_threshold\", Reason: \"must be between 0.001 and 1.0\"}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate tau_s: 1-600 seconds\n\tif v, ok := settings[\"tau_s\"]; ok {\n\t\tif f, ok := asFloat64(v); !ok || f < 1 || f > 600 {\n\t\t\treturn &ValidationError{Key: \"tau_s\", Reason: \"must be between 1 and 600\"}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate fresnel_decay: 1.0-4.0\n\tif v, ok := settings[\"fresnel_decay\"]; ok {\n\t\tif f, ok := asFloat64(v); !ok || f < 1.0 || f > 4.0 {\n\t\t\treturn &ValidationError{Key: \"fresnel_decay\", Reason: \"must be between 1.0 and 4.0\"}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate n_subcarriers: 8-47\n\tif v, ok := settings[\"n_subcarriers\"]; ok {\n\t\tif f, ok := asFloat64(v); !ok || f < 8 || f > 47 {\n\t\t\treturn &ValidationError{Key: \"n_subcarriers\", Reason: \"must be between 8 and 47\"}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate breathing_sensitivity: 0.001-0.1\n\tif v, ok := settings[\"breathing_sensitivity\"];
